content.claim: The Finnish comprehensive school reform of 1972–1977 was deliberately piloted first in rural areas as proof-of-concept for equality before scaling to urban regions, treating rural viability as a precondition of system legitimacy rather than a residual problem.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Historical institutional documentation; no quantitative enrollment/outcome comparison between pilot and later regions
Causality of sequencing choice (whether rural-first design actually produced better rural outcomes) not established in provided sources
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
